tools.name: get_token
tools.description: Authenticate with CropManage API and obtain access token. Required before any other API calls.

tools.name: get_ranches
tools.description: Fetch all ranches from CropManage API. Requires authentication token.

tools.name: get_plantings
tools.description: Fetch all plantings for a specific ranch. Requires authentication token and ranch GUID.

tools.name: get_irrigation_details
tools.description: Fetch irrigation event details and automatically load into displayRecords format. Returns enriched irrigation records ready for dashboard display.

tools.name: load_into_displayRecords
tools.description: Fetch irrigation events and transform to displayRecords JSON format for dashboard. Updates data source to 'api' mode.

tools.name: post_new_irrigation
tools.description: POST a new irrigation event to CropManage.

tools.name: update_irrigation
tools.description: Update an existing irrigation event in CropManage.

tools.name: batch_post_queue
tools.description: Process all pending irrigation records (status=-1). Automatically POSTs new records and UPDATEs existing records.

records.description: Array of irrigation records to process

tools.name: json_write
tools.description: Write (create/overwrite) a JSON file on the MCP server under the data directory.

filepath.description: Relative path under data dir, e.g. 'irrigation.json' or 'tables/users.json'

tools.name: json_read
tools.description: Read a JSON file from the MCP server data directory.

filepath.description: Relative path under data dir, e.g. 'irrigation.json'

tools.name: json_update
tools.description: Update a JSON file by shallow-merging an object into the existing JSON (objects merge; arrays replace).

updates.description: Updates to merge into existing JSON

tools.name: json_delete
tools.description: Delete a JSON file from the MCP server data directory.

tools.name: json_list
tools.description: List JSON files in a directory under the MCP server data directory.

tools.name: json_exists
tools.description: Check whether a JSON file exists in the MCP server data directory.

tools.name: data_operation
tools.description: Generic JSON table operation using {action, table, data}. Persists to <table>.json in server data dir.
